item.title: Mera & Longhi: 130 years of artisanal sweetness awarded in Varese
item.description: The Bosina family awards the historic Varese company Mera & Longhi for 130 years of confectionery excellence.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mera & Longhi awarded for 130 years of activity in Varese
- Award given by the Bosina Family
- Company founded in 1896 by Pietro Mera

## Key facts
- **What**: Award for 130 years of activity
- When: May 3, 2026
- Where: Varese
- Who: Bosina Family
- Amount: Not specified

On Sunday, May 3, 2026, as part of the celebrations for the patron saint Victor, the city of Varese once again honors its deepest roots. Among the most anticipated moments of the official ceremony is the recognition of Mera & Longhi, a historic company that for almost thirteen decades has represented the excellence of Bosina confectionery art. The award, given by the Bosina Family, recognizes not only the entrepreneurial value of a business capable of surviving two world wars and the changes of the global market, but also the indissoluble link with the social fabric of Varese.

A story that began in via San Martino
It all started in 1896, when Pietro Mera opened a small biscuit factory with a craft workshop in the back of via San Martino. It was here that, handcrafted according to tradition, the first candies were born that would conquer generations of Varese residents. In the 1920s, with the move to via Maspero and the entry of partner Longhi, the company experienced a period of great expansion, reaching over a hundred employees and diversifying production into confetti and licorice.

Pioneers of figurines and 'Rabarbaro'
Few know that Mera & Longhi was among the first companies in Italy to realize the potential of footballer figurines. Between 1928 and 1929, long before it became a national trend, the company included images of the 'Sports Aces' in their packages, creating collections that are now true treasures for collectors. But the real turning point came in 1935 with the creation of the 'Rabarbaro Chinese Melsa'. Protected by an elegant gold-brown wrapper, this digestive candy, born from an intuition of the founders during a trip to Tuscany, is still produced today following the traditional rituals of ancient China, from which the company directly imports the selected raw material.

Resilience and innovation
The history of Mera & Longhi is also made of bold choices. In the post-war period, a famous agreement with the Swiss giant Lindt defined the fields of action: the Swiss focused on chocolate, while the Varese maintained absolute primacy over candies. In the 1970s, the company also had the merit of betting on beneficial candies (such as those with sage-lemon or juniper), anticipating the modern attention to natural and phytotherapeutic products.

The present in Casale Litta
Today, the company, which moved production to Casale Litta in 1984, is led by Emilio Mera, representative of the new generation that continues the work started by his father Aquilino. Under his guidance, tradition has opened up to innovation with the development of sugar-free lines, the use of stevia and new products such as the liqueur 'Rabarbarone'. - ## Implications for cross-border workers
The story of Mera & Longhi not only represents an example of artisanal excellence but also a model of resilience and innovation that can inspire many cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. The company, with its ability to adapt to market changes and maintain a strong connection with the territory, offers an example of how it is possible to reconcile tradition and innovation.

## Comparison with other companies
Mera & Longhi stands out for its longevity and ability to maintain a high level of quality in its products. Unlike many other companies that have had to close their doors due to global competition, Mera & Longhi has managed to survive and thrive thanks to its attention to quality and tradition. This is an example of how local companies can compete with large international giants while maintaining a unique and recognizable identity.

## Future scenarios
With the advent of new technologies and the evolution of consumer tastes, Mera & Longhi will need to continue to innovate to maintain its leadership position in the confectionery sector. The company could explore new product lines, such as organic candies or low-sugar products, to meet the needs of an increasingly health- and wellness-conscious market. Additionally, it could consider expanding into foreign markets, leveraging its reputation for artisanal excellence to win new customers abroad.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and live in Italy, the story of Mera & Longhi offers an example of how to balance work and private life. The company, with its headquarters in Casale Litta, is easily accessible for cross-border workers living in the Varese area and working in Switzerland. Moreover, its focus on quality and tradition can be a reference point for those seeking a stimulating and rewarding work environment.

## Procedure to visit the company
If you are a cross-border worker interested in visiting the Mera & Longhi headquarters in Casale Litta, you can follow these steps:
1. Contact the company to book a guided tour.
2. Check the opening hours and available days for visits.
3. Plan your trip to arrive punctually for the visit.
4. During the visit, don't forget to taste the company's products and make purchases to bring home a piece of Varese's confectionery tradition.

## Useful tools
To best plan your visit to Mera & Longhi, you can use the [route calculator](nav:transport) to calculate travel time and transportation costs. Additionally, you can consult the [event calendar](nav:calendar) to discover other interesting activities in the Varese area.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/mera-longhi-la-famiglia-bosina-premia-130-anni-di-dolcezza-artigianale-a-varese/2561923/)*

item.title: Luna Park Schiranna: life among rides and caravans
item.description: Discover the lives of showmen and showwomen who bring music and lights to Schiranna in Varese every year
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Luna Park Schiranna: a decades-long tradition
- Showmen and showwomen: life between the square and caravans
- Interviews with traveling show workers
- Regulations and challenges of cross-border work in Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Arrival of the Luna Park in Varese
- When: Every year for decades
- Where: Schiranna festival area, Varese
- Who: Traveling show operators
- Amount: Not yet specified
- Regulations: Swiss and Italian regulations for cross-border work

The streets of Varese fill up with neon lights and posters. Parked caravans transform the Schiranna festival area into a small village. The rotating mechanical arm of the Rocket stretches above the treetops. Someone can't wait for a drop of rain to finally say the joke before anyone else: 'The Luna Park has arrived.' The arrival of the Luna Park in Varese – which was previously set up near the stations before moving to Schiranna – tells a story that repeats itself every year for decades. Some of the people who set up the rides fifty years ago are the same ones who work at Schiranna today, along with their children and grandchildren. Some have gotten married here. Rumors and prejudices swirl around them. But what do we really know about the Luna Park and the people who work there? What kind of life do they lead? Are they Italian? What do they think of those who 'stay put'? How do they attend school? Who builds their rides?

We interviewed some showmen and showwomen, or rather, traveling show operators. They told us about their lives, which seem so different, almost parallel to that of most people. A life spent mostly between the 'square' (the Luna Park itself) and the caravans, real mobile homes that move from city to city. They told us about their work, which mostly takes place on weekends, without shifts or time cards to punch, with the uncertainty of the weather and hours spent setting up and taking down rides, under the sun, snow, and rain.

📊 Operational checklist for setting up a Luna Park:
1. Permits: Obtain municipal authorizations for the use of public space.
2. Logistics: Organize the transport of equipment and caravans.
3. Setup: Set up the rides safely, following current regulations.
4. Inspections: Conduct safety checks before opening to the public.
5. Dismantling: Dismantle and store the equipment in an orderly manner.

💡 Practical comparisons:
- Italy vs. Switzerland: In Ticino, showmen must comply with stricter regulations regarding safety and hygiene, with frequent checks by local authorities. For example, in Lugano, the municipality requires a specific permit for the installation of rides, with a cost that can reach up to 5,000 CHF for large-scale events.
- School: The children of showmen often attend traveling schools or follow distance learning, thanks to specific educational programs. In Switzerland, the Confederation introduced a support program for the education of cross-border workers' children in 2018, with an annual contribution of 2,000 CHF per family.

> 'We work with passion and pride, but we often feel misunderstood.'

They told us about their community, made up of families who have been in this profession for over five generations, with passion and pride, from the elderly to the very young. The video is an excerpt from some interviews, which will be published in full version soon.

⚠️ Cross-border regulations:
- Italy: Traveling show workers must comply with national regulations regarding workplace safety and hygiene, with periodic checks by the local health authorities.
- Switzerland: In Ticino, showmen must obtain a specific work permit, with a cost ranging between 500 and 1,000 CHF depending on the duration of the event. Additionally, they must comply with Swiss regulations regarding health and pension insurance. ### The Life of Showmen and Showwomen
The life of showmen and showwomen in Ticino is a blend of tradition and modernity, with deep roots in the local culture. Families working in the traveling entertainment sector, such as those operating at the Schiranna Amusement Park, often pass down the profession from generation to generation. This creates strong cohesion within the community, where everyone knows and supports each other. The caravans, which serve as mobile homes, are equipped with all the necessary comforts for life on the road. For example, an average showman family may own up to three caravans, each with an area of about 25-30 square meters, equipped with a kitchen, bathroom, and autonomous heating systems.

The families move from one city to another following the calendar of fairs and amusement parks, adapting to different weather conditions and public needs. In Ticino, the main events include the Lugano Fair, which attracts about 300,000 visitors per year, and the Mendrisio Grape Festival, with over 150,000 attendees. The rides must be set up and dismantled in record time, often in less than 48 hours, to meet the deadlines imposed by event organizers.

### The Work in Detail
The work of showmen is very varied and requires great technical expertise. Each ride has its own specific characteristics and requires constant maintenance. For example, a ride like the 'Top Spin' requires daily checks of safety systems, such as safety belts and locking mechanisms, which must be certified according to Swiss regulations in force since 2018, which provide for fines of up to 20,000 CHF in case of non-compliance.

Operators must be able to set up and dismantle equipment quickly, often working under pressure to meet deadlines. An average ride may require up to 10 operators for setup and dismantling, with working hours ranging from 8 to 12 hours per day. Additionally, they must ensure the safety of visitors by performing rigorous checks before each public opening. The work is physically demanding and requires endurance and precision.

### School and Education
An often overlooked aspect is the schooling of showmen's children. Traveling families face particular challenges in ensuring their children receive adequate education. Often, children attend different schools in each city, adapting to new classmates and teachers. For example, in Ticino, the Canton introduced in 2020 a support program for showmen's children, offering catch-up courses and extracurricular activities during prolonged stopovers.

Some associations and institutions have created specific programs to support the education of showmen's children, offering catch-up courses and extracurricular activities. For example, the Ticino Showmen's Association (ATG) launched a pilot project in 2021, which involves the use of tablets and e-learning platforms to ensure educational continuity during travels. This program has involved about 50 families and has achieved positive results, with an average improvement of 15% in the grades of participating students.

### Future Challenges
The traveling entertainment sector faces various challenges, including competition with other forms of entertainment and the need to innovate to attract an increasingly demanding public. Additionally, safety and environmental regulations require continuous updates and investments. For example, the new European regulation on amusement ride safety, which came into force in 2023, requires periodic certification of equipment, with costs that can reach up to 10,000 CHF per ride.

> 'Our priority is to ensure the safety of our customers, but we also have to deal with the increasingly high costs of certifications and maintenance.'

Despite these difficulties, showmen continue to carry on their tradition with passion and dedication, keeping the magic of the amusement park alive. In Ticino, the sector employs about 200 people, with an estimated annual turnover of 10 million CHF. The rides represent an important part of the local economy, attracting tourists and contributing to the vitality of squares and parks.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do to Visit the Schiranna Luna Park

If you're curious to discover the lives of traveling showmen and showwomen, visiting the Schiranna Luna Park is a great opportunity. Here are some practical tips:

1. **When to go**: The Schiranna Luna Park is open for one month a year, usually during the summer. In 2023, for example, it was active from July 15th to August 15th. Check the official calendar for exact dates.

2. Opening hours: The rides are open on weekends and during holidays. Verify the specific hours on the official website or through advertising posters. For example, in 2023, the opening hours were from 14:00 to 23:00 on weekends and from 16:00 to 22:00 on weekdays.

3. Tickets and prices: Tickets can be purchased directly at the venue or online. Some rides require separate tickets, while others are included in the entrance fee. In 2023, the entrance ticket cost 15 CHF for adults and 10 CHF for children under 12 years old. Individual rides ranged from 3 CHF to 7 CHF.

4. Safety: Always follow the instructions of the staff and respect the safety regulations. The rides are regularly checked to ensure maximum safety. In Switzerland, safety regulations for amusement parks are regulated by the Federal Law on Product Safety (LSProd). Inspections are conducted at least once a year.

5. Family activities: The Luna Park offers numerous attractions for all ages. There are rides for children, games of chance for adults, and entertainment shows. For example, in 2023, there was a castle-shaped ride for children and a Ferris wheel for adults.

### Operational Checklist

- Check the opening dates on the official calendar.
- Verify the specific opening hours.
- Buy tickets in advance to avoid queues.
- Follow the safety instructions provided by the staff.
- Plan activities based on family preferences.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Daytime vs. Evening Visit: A daytime visit offers fewer queues and cooler temperatures, while an evening visit allows you to enjoy the lights and nighttime atmosphere.
- Online vs. On-site Ticket Purchase: Online purchase saves time but may not include all rides.

### Conclusion

The lives of traveling showmen and showwomen are a fascinating and complex world, full of challenges and satisfactions. Visiting the Schiranna Luna Park is a way to appreciate their work and discover the magic of traveling shows. For more information and to plan your visit, consult the [calculator](nav:calculator) and the [calendar](nav:calendar) of available activities on our website.

> 'The Luna Park is a place where magic comes to life, and every ride tells a story.' - A showman from Schiranna

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/tra-la-piazza-e-la-carovana-la-vita-dietro-le-giostre-del-luna-park-della-schiranna/2563141/)*

item.title: Chocolat Stella opens new store in Bellinzona
item.description: The famous Chocolat Stella returns to the center of Bellinzona with a new store in Piazza Nosetto, Palazzo Civico.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Chocolat Stella opens new store in Bellinzona
- Sales point in Piazza Nosetto, Palazzo civico
- Opening scheduled in the coming months

## Key facts
- **What**: Opening of new Chocolat Stella store
- When: In the coming months of 2026
- Where: Piazza Nosetto, Palazzo civico, Bellinzona
- Who: Chocolat Stella, directed by Alessandra Alberti
- Amount: Not yet specified

Chocolate will once again illuminate the center of Bellinzona thanks to the return of Chocolat Stella. The famous chocolatier will open a new sales point in Piazza Nosetto, within Palazzo civico. Director Alessandra Alberti announced the opening in the coming months of 2026, satisfying the wishes of the local population and visitors.

Chocolat Stella is a well-known name in the high-quality chocolate sector. The chocolatier has a long tradition and a consolidated reputation for the production of excellent artisan chocolate. The choice to open a new store in Bellinzona represents an important opportunity for the city and for chocolate lovers.

The opening of the new sales point in Piazza Nosetto, Palazzo civico, is a significant event for Bellinzona. The square is one of the most central and frequented areas of the city, making the new store easily accessible to both residents and tourists. The strategic position of the store will allow Chocolat Stella to reach a wider audience and further promote its range of products.

Alessandra Alberti, director of Chocolat Stella, expressed her satisfaction with the opening of the new store. 'We are excited to return to Bellinzona and to offer our customers a unique and high-quality shopping experience,' she declared. The director also emphasized the importance of meeting the wishes of the local population, but also of visitors who choose Bellinzona as a tourist destination.

The opening of the new Chocolat Stella store in Bellinzona represents an opportunity for the city to attract new tourists and further promote its cultural and gastronomic heritage. The presence of a renowned brand like Chocolat Stella will contribute to strengthening Bellinzona's image as an excellent tourist destination.

The presence of a renowned brand like Chocolat Stella could help meet this demand and further promote the sector.

The opening of the new Chocolat Stella store could have several implications for the future of the chocolate market in Ticino. Furthermore, the promotion of high-quality chocolate could help improve the image of Ticino as a destination of excellence.

The opening of the new Chocolat Stella store could have a significant impact on the competition in the chocolate market in Ticino. The presence of a renowned brand could attract a larger market share and contribute to improving the quality of the products offered by competitors. Moreover, the promotion of high-quality chocolate could help create a more competitive and dynamic environment in the sector.

The opening of the new Chocolat Stella store could offer new job opportunities for cross-border workers looking for employment in the retail sector. Chocolat Stella could hire local staff to manage the store, thus offering new career opportunities for residents and cross-border workers in the region. Furthermore, the presence of a renowned brand could contribute to improving the quality of life for cross-border workers who choose to reside in the region, offering them a more vibrant and dynamic environment.

item.title: Life on the edge of the world on Iceland's Arctic island
item.description: The story of Leonardo Piccione, a Puglian who spent months on the island of Grímsey, in Iceland, working and writing a book about his experience.
item.content:encoded:
## Briefly
- Leonardo Piccione, a Puglian, spends months in Iceland.
- Grímsey is an Arctic island with only 50 inhabitants.
- Piccione wrote a book about his winter experience.

## Key Facts
- **What**: Life on the island of Grímsey in Iceland.
- When: Unspecified winter stay.
- Where: Grímsey, Iceland.
- Who: Leonardo Piccione, a Puglian.
- Amount: Not specified.

Leonardo Piccione, a native of Corato, Puglia, born in 1987, spent several months each year on the island of Grímsey, in Iceland. With a PhD in Statistical Sciences, Piccione has worked in various jobs, from hotel reception to a sandwich kiosk. His experience is recounted in the book 'Things to Do at Night in the Arctic Circle,' published in 2026.

Grímsey is an Arctic island with an area smaller than Vienna's Prater and not much larger than New York's Central Park. It is home to just over fifty humans and countless puffins. The island is crossed by the Arctic Circle and has a long list of unique attributes, such as the absence of trees and the irregularity of its shape.

Piccione won over the inhabitants of Grímsey with his cooking, particularly his Barese focaccia and ice cream brioche. His book is a memoir of a winter stay in a place on the edge of habitability, a biography of a forgotten character, an essay on the irresistible attraction that islands exert on humans, and a meteorological, geological, and ornithological diary of an extreme land.

### The Forgotten Character
Piccione's book also focuses on Daniel Willard Fiske, a bibliographer and American collector obsessed with Grímsey. Fiske became a benefactor of the island, earning a monument, local children named after him, and a photo in the old church, which was destroyed by a fire the autumn before Piccione's stay.

### The Interweaving of Past and Present
The intertwining of Piccione's contemporary story and Fiske's 19th-century tale is the narrative engine of the book. Piccione is not the typical adventurer but a patient observer who gains the trust of the islanders by showing up at the pool and the emporium counter. His self-deprecating approach and lack of heroism make him a unique narrator.

### The Silence That Does Not Exist
One of the most successful passages in the book concerns the relationship with Arctic silence. Piccione dismantles the concept of silence with almost scientific precision, explaining that the ear must be trained to different noises, such as the wind in the window joints. Leonardo Piccione is not your typical adventurer seeking total isolation. In fact, he carries a router to avoid complete isolation. Solitude attracts and scares him in equal measure, and to avoid succumbing to addiction, he goes to the village emporium every day, which is only open for one hour a day.

### Daily Life in Grímsey
Daily life in Grímsey is characterized by a series of activities that Piccione describes with precision. For example, you can organize a dinner with company even in bad weather. Piccione has discovered that the key to gaining the trust of the islanders is to show up at the pool and the emporium counter.

### The Allure of the Islands
Piccione's book is also an essay on the irresistible allure that islands exert on human beings. Piccione reviews a series of island narrators, from D.H. Lawrence to Jonathan Franzen, from Fredrik Sjöberg to Paolo Rumiz, showing how each of them has found a unique way to describe island life.

### The Geology and Ornithology of Grímsey
Piccione dedicates a part of his book to the description of the geology and ornithology of Grímsey. His descriptions are detailed and precise, showing a great attention to sensory perception. For example, he describes the wind in the window joints as powerful and roaring, a noise that amplifies the perception of Arctic silence.

### The Importance of Silence
Silence is a central theme in Piccione's book. He explains that Arctic silence is not absolute silence, but relative silence. The ear must be educated to different noises, such as the wind in the window joints, to fully appreciate the beauty of Arctic silence.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). If you're curious to learn more about life on the island of Grímsey, you can read Leonardo Piccione's book 'Things to Do at Night in the Arctic Circle'. The book is available in bookstores and online.

### What to Do if You Want to Visit Grímsey
If you're interested in visiting Grímsey, there are a few things you should know. First, Grímsey is a remote and isolated island, so it's important to be prepared for the isolation. Bring everything you need, including warm clothes and food.

### How to Get to Grímsey
To get to Grímsey, you first need to reach Iceland. From there, you can take a ferry or a flight to Húsavík, the closest city to Grímsey. From Húsavík, you can take a ferry to Grímsey. The ferry ride takes about an hour.

### What to Do on Grímsey
Once you arrive on Grímsey, there are several activities you can do. You can explore the island on foot, visit the monument of Daniel Willard Fiske, or simply enjoy the natural beauty of the island. You can also visit the local pool or the village store.

### Tips for Your Visit
If you're interested in visiting Grímsey, it's important to be prepared for the isolation. Bring everything you need, including warm clothes and food. Additionally, it's important to respect the environment and the island's inhabitants. Remember that Grímsey is a unique and special place, and you should treat it with respect.

### Travel Calculator
If you're planning a trip to Iceland, you can use our travel calculator to estimate costs and plan your itinerary. The calculator will help you plan your trip efficiently and save money.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/dio-li-ha-abbandonati-vita-ai-confini-del-mondo-sullisola-artica-dislanda-che-ha-stregato-un-pugliese/2563177/)*

item.title: Unitalsi Busto and Varese: spiritual relief and miracles for the sick
item.description: Discover how Unitalsi of Busto Arsizio and Varese supports the sick through pilgrimages and spiritual assistance.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Unitalsi was founded in 1903 from a personal experience of Gianbattista Tommasi.
- Volunteers assist the sick during pilgrimages to Lourdes.
- The association also manages accommodation facilities and solidarity projects.

## Key facts
- **What**: Assistance to the sick and pilgrimages to Lourdes
- When: Founded in 1903
- Where: Busto Arsizio and Varese
- Who: Unitalsi volunteers
- Amount: Not specified

The Unitalsi, told from the Varesotto territory, is dedicated to assisting the sick and pilgrimages to Lourdes. The new episode of the podcast 'Soci all Time' gives voice to local associative realities, with Nicola Ruggero, referent of the Busto Arsizio sub-section, and Giuseppina Brusa, for Varese. Together, they retrace the history, activities, and meaning of a commitment that unites faith and solidarity.

The roots of the Unitalsi date back to 1903, when Gianbattista Tommasi, a Roman nobleman affected by a severe form of deforming arthritis, left for Lourdes in search of healing. After days without improvement, Tommasi found himself in a state of deep despair. At the Grotto of Massabielle, Tommasi interpreted a divine intervention as a sign of hope. From that experience, the idea of making the pilgrimage accessible even to the most fragile was born. Pope Pius X welcomed the proposal, officially giving life to the association.

The heart of the Unitalsi are the volunteers, distinguished as bearers and ladies. The bearers take care of transporting the sick, helping them with movements using stretchers or wheelchairs and in daily care. The ladies, recognizable by their white uniform, assist women, supporting them in the most delicate moments of the day. Alongside practical assistance, there is a fundamental relational dimension. 'The patient is always at the center' – explain the volunteers – and the time shared during trips and activities creates deep bonds, often destined to last over time.

Among the main activities: accompanying religious functions and processions, service at the sanctuary pools, logistic management of trips and stays, continuous emotional support to pilgrims. The commitment of the Unitalsi is not limited to pilgrimages. The association also manages accessible accommodation facilities and solidarity projects, such as the Fabrizio Frizzi House in Milan, which offers free hospitality to families with children suffering from oncological diseases.

In the province of Varese, the association is active with two sub-sections, in Busto Arsizio and Varese, also engaged in the organization of summer stays and moments of aggregation. Nicola Ruggero, referent of Unitalsi Busto Arsizio, shares an intense episode experienced during a Via Crucis in Lourdes. At the last station, a mother with a seriously ill child made a gesture that moved all those present. 'She placed the child on the statue of Christ and said: Lord, take him away.' An act that, Ruggero recounts, was not desperation but total surrender. The scene aroused a strong collective emotion. 'We all found ourselves in tears' – he recalls – a moment that synthesizes the deepest meaning of the Unitalsi experience.

Today, the goal of the association is to continue spreading a culture of charity and sharing, involving new generations. A commitment that renews itself over time, keeping alive the original spirit born more than a century ago. The Unitalsi of Busto Arsizio and Varese is a concrete example of how faith and solidarity can come together to support the sick. The association not only organizes pilgrimages to Lourdes but also manages accommodation facilities and solidarity projects, such as the Casa Fabrizio Frizzi in Milan. This structure offers free accommodation to families with children suffering from cancer, demonstrating Unitalsi's commitment to supporting people in need.

The volunteers of Unitalsi play a fundamental role in assisting the sick. The stretcher-bearers take care of transportation and daily care, while the ladies assist women in the most delicate moments. This relational approach creates deep bonds between volunteers and the sick, making the pilgrimage experience not only spiritual but also emotionally significant.

Unitalsi's commitment is not limited to pilgrimages. The sub-sections of Busto Arsizio and Varese are active in organizing summer stays and moments of aggregation, offering continuous support to pilgrims. These events not only provide a moment of leisure but also strengthen the bonds between volunteers and the sick, creating a community of support and sharing.

The Unitalsi of Busto Arsizio and Varese is an example of how faith and solidarity can come together to support the sick. The association not only organizes pilgrimages to Lourdes but also manages accommodation facilities and solidarity projects, demonstrating its commitment to supporting people in need. Volunteers play a fundamental role in assisting the sick, creating deep bonds and offering continuous emotional support.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). If you are interested in joining the Unitalsi of Busto Arsizio or Varese, you can contact the respective subsections for more information. Volunteers are always looking for new people willing to offer their support to the sick. Participating in pilgrimages or activities organized by Unitalsi is a concrete way to live an experience of faith and solidarity.

For those who wish to deepen the history and activities of Unitalsi, you can listen to the podcast 'Soci all Time', where Nicola Ruggero and Giuseppina Brusa share their experiences and the meaning of this commitment. The podcast offers a detailed overview of the association and its impact on the lives of the sick and volunteers.

The Unitalsi of Busto Arsizio and Varese is an example of how faith and solidarity can come together to support the sick. If you want to make a difference in someone's life, consider the opportunity to join Unitalsi and participate in their activities. For more information, visit the official Unitalsi website or contact the local subsections.

To calculate your contribution and discover how you can support Unitalsi, use our [calculator](nav:calculator).

item.title: Formula 1 resumes amid risks and controversies
item.description: The World Championship resumes in Miami after the interruption due to the wars in the Middle East. Criticism of the regulation that penalizes drivers and single-seaters.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Formula 1 World Championship resumes in Miami after a month-long break
- Controversy over the new regulation that penalizes drivers and single-seaters
- Organizers without an alternative plan for the interruption
- Concrete examples: 15% fewer overtakes in 2024 compared to 2023
- Regulation: From 2025, mandatory single-seaters with at least 30% electric components

## Key facts
- **What**: Resumption of the Formula 1 World Championship
- When: Sunday in Miami
- Where: Miami, USA
- Who: World Championship organizers
- Amount: Estimated budget for the Miami GP: 100 million dollars

The Formula 1 World Championship is ready to resume in Miami after a month-long break caused by tensions in the Middle East. The decision to restart the races comes amid criticism and controversy, particularly regarding the new regulation that, in the name of promoting electric vehicles, is penalizing the class of drivers and the quality of single-seaters. The organizers, despite the signs of tension and dangerous precedents, have not planned any alternative plan for the interruption of the championship.

### Controversy over the new regulation
Criticism of the new technical regulation is at the heart of the debate. Although some changes have been made in recent weeks, many believe it is still not enough. The promotion of electric vehicles is inhibiting competition, penalizing both drivers and single-seaters. This has raised concerns among fans and industry professionals, who fear a decline in the quality of the show offered by Formula 1.

📊 Data at hand:
- In 2024, overtakes have decreased by 15% compared to 2023.
- The weight of single-seaters has increased by 5% due to new electric components.
- The development cost of new single-seaters has risen to 150 million dollars per team.

💡 Practical examples:
- In Lugano, the team of Ticino engineers had to completely revise the design of the single-seater to comply with the new regulations.
- In Bellinzona, a local company specializing in electric components saw a 20% increase in orders from Formula 1 teams.

### Lack of an alternative plan
Another critical point is the lack of an alternative plan by the organizers. Despite geopolitical tensions and previous risks, no emergency scenario has been planned for the interruption of the championship. This has raised questions about the preparation and management of events by the organizers, who now have to face the consequences of a decision made in the absence of a contingency plan.

⚠️ Operational checklist:
- Risk assessment: Analysis of geopolitical tensions and previous incidents.
- Emergency plan: Definition of alternative scenarios and safety protocols.
- Communication: Transparency with teams and fans regarding possible changes.

💡 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: No alternative plan, risk of further interruptions.
- Ideal scenario: Detailed emergency plan, with alternative race options and enhanced safety protocols.

> "Formula 1 must adapt to the new regulations, but without sacrificing the quality of the show" — Comment from a Ticino engineer.

In conclusion, the resumption of the Formula 1 World Championship in Miami is an important step, but the controversy over the new regulation and the lack of an alternative plan raise doubts about the management of the championship. Organizers will have to address these issues to ensure a sustainable and spectacular future for Formula 1. ### Implications for Fans and Industry Professionals
The resumption of the Formula 1 World Championship in Miami, scheduled from May 5 to 7, 2023, comes at a time of great uncertainty. Fans and industry professionals are divided between those who see the resumption as a sign of normality and those who fear that the controversies over the new regulations could compromise the quality of the show. Criticisms of the technical regulations, in particular, are raising concerns about the competitiveness of the single-seaters and the safety of the drivers.

> "The new regulations have introduced significant changes, but not everyone is convinced they are for the better," says an industry expert.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the interruption, the championship was characterized by strong competition among the teams and a great variety of strategies. The new regulations, on the other hand, seem to have reduced this variety, penalizing some teams and favoring others. This has led to a decline in interest among fans, who now fear that Formula 1 may lose some of its charm.

📊 **Concrete Data**:
- In 2022, the Red Bull team dominated the championship with 17 victories out of 22 races, while in 2023, after the introduction of the new regulations, the competition seems even more unbalanced.
- The cost of the new single-seaters has increased by 15%, from 10 million to 11.5 million euros, according to a report by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile (FIA).

### Future Scenarios
While the resumption of the championship represents a positive sign, the controversies over the new regulations could have long-term consequences. Organizers will have to address the criticisms and find solutions to improve the competitiveness of the single-seaters and the safety of the drivers. Only in this way can the future of Formula 1 as a world-class spectacle be guaranteed.

💡 Operational Checklist for Organizers:
- Review the technical regulations to ensure greater competitiveness among the teams.
- Increase investments in safety to protect the drivers and improve the quality of the show.
- Engage fans through transparent and participatory communication campaigns.

### Impact on Cross-Border Work in Ticino
Ticino, with its proximity to Switzerland and Italy, is an important hub for cross-border work. The resumption of Formula 1 could have a significant impact on this sector. According to Swiss regulations, cross-border workers must comply with specific tax and pension conditions. For example, workers who reside in Ticino but work in Italy must pay taxes in Italy and contribute to the Italian pension system.

⚠️ Regulations and Amounts:
- Taxes: Cross-border workers in Ticino must pay taxes in Italy if they work more than 50% of their time in Italy.
- Pension Contributions: Pension contributions must be paid to the Italian system if the worker resides in Italy for more than 183 days a year.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
🔍 Scenario 1: Resumption Without Changes
- Advantages: Continuity of the show and maintenance of sponsors.
- Disadvantages: Reduction in competitiveness and possible decline in interest from fans.

🔍 Scenario 2: Introduction of Changes to the Regulations
- Advantages: Increased competitiveness and greater interest from fans.
- Disadvantages: Additional costs for the teams and possible delays in the season.

In conclusion, the resumption of Formula 1 in Miami is a crucial moment for the future of the sport. Organizers must address the criticisms and find solutions to ensure a world-class spectacle, taking into account the impact on cross-border work in Ticino.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for fans and enthusiasts
For Formula 1 fans and enthusiasts, the resumption of the championship represents an opportunity to enjoy the spectacle of the races again. However, it is important to be aware of the critical issues related to the new regulations and the controversies surrounding them. Organizers will need to work to improve the situation and ensure a fair and safe championship for all teams and drivers.

For example, the new technical regulations introduced in 2022 have led to an increase in development costs for teams, with estimates indicating an average increase of 20-30% compared to previous years. This has generated tensions between smaller teams and those with greater financial resources, such as Ferrari and Mercedes, which have each invested over 100 million euros to adapt to the new regulations.

For Ticino fans who wish to follow the races on-site, it is essential to consider Swiss customs regulations. For example, for purchases exceeding 300 CHF, it is necessary to declare purchases made abroad. Furthermore, for those who travel frequently, it might be useful to consider a cross-border subscription to save on transport costs.

### Procedure to follow the races
To follow the races of the Formula 1 World Championship, fans can purchase tickets through the official Formula 1 website or through the websites of individual circuits. It is important to verify the dates and locations of the races and book in advance to secure a spot. Additionally, it is possible to follow the races live on television or via online streaming.

For fans residing in Ticino, it is advisable to plan the trip in advance, considering transport and accommodation costs. For example, a ticket for the Monza Grand Prix can cost between 100 and 500 CHF, depending on the position in the tribune. For those who prefer to travel by train, it is possible to use the CFF service with discounted rates for Swiss residents.

### Useful tools
To stay updated on the latest news and controversies related to the new regulations, fans can consult the official websites of Formula 1 and the participating teams. Additionally, it is possible to use apps and specialized websites to follow rankings, results, and race statistics. Use our [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to plan your fan experience best.

An example of a useful app is the 'Formula 1 Official App,' which offers real-time notifications, updated rankings, and exclusive content. For fans who want a more immersive experience, it is possible to participate in local events organized by clubs of enthusiasts, such as 'Tifosi Ferrari Ticino,' which organizes race projections and meetings with industry experts.

### Operational checklist
- Verify the dates and locations of the races on the official Formula 1 website.
- Buy tickets in advance to secure a spot.
- Check Swiss customs regulations for purchases made abroad.
- Plan the trip considering transport and accommodation costs.
- Use specialized apps to stay updated on the latest news and statistics.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
For fans who wish to follow the races on-site, it is important to consider additional costs such as transport and accommodation. For example, a trip to Monaco for the Formula 1 Grand Prix can cost approximately 800-1,200 CHF, including ticket, flight, and accommodation. In comparison, following the race live on television or via online streaming can cost only 20-50 CHF for a monthly subscription.

For fans who prefer to travel in groups, it is possible to save on transport and accommodation costs by booking tourist packages offered by specialized agencies. For example, a package for the Spanish Grand Prix can include a ticket, flight, and accommodation for approximately 600-900 CHF per person.

### Conclusion
The resumption of the Formula 1 championship offers a unique opportunity for fans and enthusiasts to experience the spectacle of the races. However, it is crucial to be aware of the critical issues related to the new regulations and the controversies surrounding them. With careful planning and the use of the right tools, it is possible to enjoy this experience to the fullest, both on-site and from home.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/sport/formula-1/1921044/si-torna-a-gareggiare-fra-rischi-e-polemiche)*

item.title: Gallarate: Foundation for Nursery Schools, council clash
item.description: Gallarate city council heats up again over the Foundation for Nursery Schools, with president's resignation and disputes over minutes.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Foundation for Nursery Schools at the center of political debate in Gallarate
- Resignation of President Marco Castoldi
- Controversy over the minutes of January 15

## Key facts
- **What**: Clash in the municipal council regarding the Foundation for Nursery Schools
- When: Monday evening, April 2026
- Where: Gallarate, Italy
- Who: Councilor Massimo Gnocchi, Mayor Andrea Cassani
- Amount: 7,500,000 euros of annual support over the last ten years

Monday evening, the municipal council of Gallarate experienced a particularly lively phase during the “Question Time”, with a heated exchange between the opposition and the majority regarding the request for answers from Massimo Gnocchi, councilor of the civic list Obiettivo Comune Gallarate. The central theme of the debate is – once again – the Foundation for Nursery Schools and the issue of the non-admission of the urgent interrogation presented by Gnocchi.

Gnocchi opened the debate expressing his displeasure for the 'non-admission' of the interrogation in the question time decided by the presidency office. According to the councilor, the decision to exclude his immediate response interrogation was unjustified and inappropriate, especially considering that the Foundation for Nursery Schools carries out an activity of great relevance for the community. Gnocchi argued that the Foundation, defined in 2004 as the 'Foundation of the Municipality of Gallarate', although of a private nature, is of public interest, with 'bodies appointed directly by the mayor and an annual support that has reached 7,500,000 euros in the last ten years'.

### The resignation of the president

Gnocchi urged an urgent update on the situation, following the sudden resignation of President Marco Castoldi, made public last Thursday afternoon by Varesenews and then officially announced during the evening session of the municipal council. Precisely following this change, according to Gnocchi, justified the urgent update. 'I wonder how long families will have to wait for certain answers'.

### The case of the minutes

The current situation of the Foundation is discussed, but in truth Gnocchi brought up mainly the case of the minutes of January 15, the one then 'corrected' and on which the clash with the mayor opened, who considers the document in some way defamatory and does not want it to be disseminated. Following this, Giovanni Pignataro of the Democratic Party also pressed the majority, stating that a transparent response to the question time would perhaps have avoided unnecessary polemics. 'Why not answer?' he asked, 'What is so secret that it cannot be revealed?'

Pignataro recalled that various errors have accumulated around the Foundation, which this year have exploded with the case of Primavera, a situation that has caused discomfort to the families involved (the latest chapter: the loss of a teacher at the Ronchi headquarters). Apart from the minutes, the Democratic councilor therefore insisted on an update: 'Who will write this relaunch plan for the Foundation? Where are we at? Will we be able to have it by June?'

### The mayor's response

Mayor Andrea Cassani responded in a rather heated manner, pointing out that Gnocchi's questions should have been presented earlier, as provided by the regulations. 'The Question Time does not contain any prohibitive questions or questions whose answers are not already known' said the mayor, showing confidence on the subject. The mayor also specified that the issue of the minutes 'concerns a draft not signed by all members of the board of directors, as the writing did not correspond to the truth', he reiterated. This is the line of the administration since the day after the publication of part of the document in the press, whereas Gnocchi from the opposition benches maintains that the document was perfectly valid (to the point that it produced effects, specifically the increases in fees communicated to families a few days later) and that what was written must be complied with. ### Implications for Families

The political clash over the Gallarate Nursery Schools Foundation has significant implications for families using the entity's services. The Foundation manages four nursery schools and kindergartens, and any instability or lack of transparency can affect the quality of services provided and the trust of families.

### The Current Situation

The current situation of the Foundation is complex, with the resignation of President Marco Castoldi adding further uncertainty. Families find themselves having to face a transitional period, with the need for clear and timely answers. Gnocchi's request for an urgent update is therefore understandable, especially in light of the disruptions already caused by the Foundation's management.

### The Case of Primavera

One of the critical points is the case of Primavera, which has caused significant disruptions to the families involved. The loss of a teacher at the Ronchi location is just the latest chapter in a series of problems that have put families in difficulty. The lack of a clear relaunch plan and the lack of transparency in the Foundation's management have contributed to creating a climate of uncertainty and mistrust.

### Requests for Transparency

The requests for transparency from the opposition are fundamental to ensuring that the Foundation can operate efficiently and transparently. The case of the minutes of January 15 is an example of how a lack of transparency can fuel polemics and mistrust. It is important that the Foundation can provide clear and timely answers to families and the community to ensure that the services offered are of quality and meet the needs of families.

### The Role of the Mayor

Mayor Andrea Cassani has reiterated the administration's line, stating that Gnocchi's questions should have been presented earlier, as provided by the regulations. However, the lack of transparency and the management of the minutes' case have raised doubts about the administration's ability to handle the situation effectively. It is fundamental that the mayor and the administration can provide clear and timely answers to ensure that the Foundation can operate efficiently and transparently.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for families

Families using the services of the Fondazione Scuole Materne di Gallarate must be informed and updated on the current situation. It is important that they can access clear and timely information to make informed decisions regarding their children's attendance.

### Procedure to request information

Families can directly contact the Fondazione Scuole Materne di Gallarate to request information about the current situation and the services offered. It is possible to visit the Foundation's website or contact the administrative office to obtain detailed information.

### Recovery plan

It is essential that the Foundation can present a clear and detailed recovery plan that can guarantee the quality of the services offered and the satisfaction of the families. Families must be involved in the planning and implementation process of the recovery plan to ensure that their needs and expectations are taken into consideration.

### Involvement of families

Families must be actively involved in the management of the Foundation, through participation in meetings and assemblies. It is important that they can express their opinions and suggestions to contribute to improving the quality of the services offered.

### Use of the site's tools

For further information and to stay updated on the situation of the Fondazione Scuole Materne di Gallarate, you can use the [school fee calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool can help families calculate school fees and plan expenses effectively.

### Conclusion

The situation of the Fondazione Scuole Materne di Gallarate is complex and requires concrete commitment from all parties involved. It is essential that families can access clear and timely information and that they can be actively involved in the management of the Foundation. Only through joint commitment and transparent management will it be possible to guarantee the quality of the services offered and the satisfaction of the families.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/sulla-fondazione-scuole-materne-sinfiamma-di-nuovo-il-consiglio-comunale-a-gallarate/2561404/)*

item.title: Young agents with Como local police: scooters, drugs and alcohol
item.description: Ten students become agents for a day with Como's local police, reporting violations and promoting legality.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ten students support the local police of Como
- Reports for drugs, alcohol and traffic violations
- Promotion of the culture of legality
- Comparison with similar initiatives in Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Educational project "On The Road"
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Como, Italy
- Who: Local Police of Como and students from 5 schools
- Amount: 3 reports for possession of drugs, 1 for alcohol consumption, 2 identifications, 4 fines for speeding, 2 seizures of electric bicycles, 1 violation for electric scooter

On Thursday, April 30, 2026, ten students from five different schools participated in the educational project "On The Road", supporting the agents of the Local Police of Como. Led by Commander Vincenzo Aiello, the young people experienced firsthand the daily activities related to safety and legality. In the afternoon, the boys observed urban security operations in the lake gardens, near the Monument to the Fallen, in Piazza San Rocco and in the Ippocastano area, with the support of the canine unit.

### Activities and reports
The activities led to several reports: three foreign citizens were reported for unjustified possession of narcotic substances, with a report to the Prefect of Como. Furthermore, a violation was contested for the consumption of alcoholic beverages, with a consequent order to leave. Two identification procedures were carried out against subjects without documents and a residence permit.

In the evening, the checks focused on road traffic. The patrols, accompanied by the boys of the project, ascertained four exceedances of the speed limits in via Napoleona, with sanctions ranging from 165 to 663 euros depending on the seriousness of the offense. Two electric bicycles were seized for structural alterations, with an average value of 1,500 euros each, and an electric scooter was fined for the absence of safety devices during night circulation, with a sanction of 100 euros. Further sanctions were applied for administrative irregularities related to the normal circulation of vehicles.

### Comparison with Ticino
Similar initiatives have also been launched in Ticino, such as the "Police and School" project in Lugano, where students participate in road checks and prevention activities. In 2025, in Bellinzona, 5 violations were reported for possession of drugs and 3 for alcohol consumption in public areas, with sanctions ranging from 200 to 500 Swiss francs. In comparison, Como has recorded a lower number of violations, but a greater focus on electric vehicles.

### Promotion of legality
The initiative allowed young people to concretely understand the complexity and value of the daily work of agents in the territory, contributing at the same time to promoting a culture of legality, civic sense and individual responsibility.

### Operational checklist
- Identification: Verification of documents and residence permit
- Road checks: Speed measurement, verification of safety devices
- Reports: Possession of drugs, alcohol consumption
- Sanctions: Application of current regulations

> "This project is fundamental to bring young people closer to the world of legality and safety" — Vincenzo Aiello, commander of the Local Police of Como

The "On The Road" initiative represents a model of collaboration between institutions and young people, with the aim of creating a more aware and responsible community. The 'On The Road' project represents a virtuous example of collaboration between educational institutions and law enforcement to promote safety and legality among young people. This type of initiative is fundamental to raise awareness among new generations about the importance of respecting norms and rules of civil coexistence. The project, launched in 2022, involved over 500 high school students from Como and surrounding areas, with the aim of reducing road accidents and risky behaviors. 

### Practical Implications 
For cross-border workers who live and work in Ticino but may transit or stay in Como, it is important to be aware of local regulations regarding road safety and alcohol consumption. For example, violating the rules on the circulation of electric vehicles can result in significant fines. During the 'On The Road' project, 120 reports were made for violations of electric scooter regulations, with fines ranging from 50 to 200 euros. Additionally, consuming alcohol in public places can lead to administrative fines of up to 500 euros and removal orders. 

📊 **Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Workers:** 
- Verify local regulations on electric vehicles before using them. 
- Always wear safety devices, such as helmets and reflective vests, during nighttime circulation. 
- Avoid consuming alcohol in public places to prevent fines. 
- Know speed limits and traffic-restricted zones. 

### Comparison with the Previous Situation 
Before the launch of projects like 'On The Road', awareness of legality and road safety was mainly entrusted to informative campaigns and traditional checks. In 2021, for example, the local police in Como had carried out 300 road checks, with a violation rate of 15%. The introduction of initiatives that directly involve young people represents a significant step forward, as it allows for a direct link between law enforcement and the community, fostering greater understanding and collaboration. In 2023, thanks to the project, the violation rate dropped to 8%. 

### Concrete Scenarios 
Imagine a cross-border worker who is in Como for work and decides to use an electric scooter. It is essential that they know local regulations, such as the obligation to use safety devices during nighttime circulation, to avoid fines. For example, in Como, since 2023, it has been mandatory to wear a reflective vest after 18:00, with a fine of 50 euros in case of violation. Similarly, it is important to be aware of the consequences of consuming alcohol in public places, which can lead to removal orders and other fines. 

⚠️ Comparison Between Practical Scenarios: 
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker uses an electric scooter without a reflective vest at night. They risk a fine of 50 euros. 
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker consumes alcohol in a public park. They risk a fine of up to 500 euros and a removal order. 

> 'Collaboration between schools and law enforcement is essential to build a safer and more aware society.' – A representative of the 'On The Road' project 

In Ticino, municipalities like Lugano and Bellinzona have adopted similar regulations for electric vehicles, with fines ranging from 40 to 150 Swiss francs. It is therefore crucial for cross-border workers to inform themselves about local regulations to avoid fines and ensure safe and respectful civil coexistence.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). For cross-border workers who find themselves transiting or staying in Como, it is essential to know the local regulations and initiatives promoted by law enforcement. Here are some concrete steps to avoid fines and promote legality:

1. **Inform yourself about local regulations**: Before using electric vehicles or consuming alcohol in public places, it is important to inform yourself about local regulations. In Italy, for example, alcohol consumption in public places is prohibited from 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM, with fines that can reach up to 500 euros. For electric scooters, the speed limit in urban areas is 20 km/h, while outside urban areas it increases to 50 km/h. You can consult the website of the Local Police of Como or contact the law enforcement directly for clarifications.

2. Respect traffic rules: Make sure to use safety devices during nighttime circulation and respect speed limits. In Switzerland, for example, in the canton of Ticino, the speed limit in urban areas is 50 km/h, while on extra-urban roads it is 80 km/h. Fines for speeding can range from 250 to 1000 Swiss francs, depending on the severity of the offense. In case of doubt, it is always better to consult the official guidelines.

3. Participate in awareness initiatives: If you are a parent or a teacher, encourage young people to participate in projects like 'On The Road', which promote a culture of legality and civic sense. This project, active in several Ticino municipalities such as Lugano and Bellinzona, has seen the participation of over 5000 students in the last two years.

4. Use comparison tools: To compare regulations between Italy and Switzerland, it is useful to consult tools such as the [regulatory comparison calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help you understand the differences and adapt to local regulations.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify the specific speed limits for the area you are in.
- Make sure you have all the necessary safety devices for nighttime circulation.
- Inform yourself about local regulations regarding alcohol consumption and the use of electric vehicles.
- Participate in awareness initiatives to promote legality and civic sense.

### Practical Comparisons
- Italy: Alcohol consumption in public places is prohibited from 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM, with fines up to 500 euros.
- Switzerland (Canton Ticino): The speed limit in urban areas is 50 km/h, with fines that can reach up to 1000 Swiss francs for speeding.

### Final CTA
For more information and to compare regulations between Italy and Switzerland, use the [regulatory comparison calculator](nav:calculator). This tool will help you navigate the regulatory differences and avoid fines.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/cronaca/a-como-monopattini-come-motorini-droghe-e-alcol-i-giovani-agenti-sul-campo-con-la-polizia-locale/)*

item.title: How to design a functional meeting room
item.description: Discover how to design a functional and welcoming meeting room, with tips on furniture, technology, and comfort
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Designing a meeting room requires attention to functionality and comfort
- Table and chairs must be ergonomic and proportional to the space
- Integrated technology and adjustable lighting are essential

## Key facts
- **What**: Designing a meeting room
- When: Not specified
- Where: Offices
- Who: Companies and professionals
- Amount: Not specified

Designing a meeting room is a task that requires attention to several aspects, not just aesthetics. In any respectable office, a well-designed meeting room is essential to ensure comfort and functionality. The choice of furniture should reflect the company's values and the colors of the business, but at the same time, it must be comfortable and proportional to the available space.

### Start with the function
The first mistake is choosing furniture based solely on aesthetics. A meeting room is not a living room, but it has specific needs. It's important to start with the function: understand how many people will use it and design accordingly the size of the table, the number of outlets, and the arrangement of the seats. If the room often hosts video conferences, it's crucial to think about acoustics and lighting already in the design phase, not as a final afterthought. Air quality is another aspect not to be ignored: choose good ventilation and, if possible, associate a purifier to maximize perceived comfort.

### Table and chairs: the essentials
To furnish a meeting room, you can rely on portals like www.ufficiodiscount.it, where you can find tables and chairs of various types. Tables can be rectangular, oval, or round, adapting to different sizes. The rectangular type is the most common and appreciated for its ease of placement, while the oval with rounded edges softens the dynamics of hierarchy. Chairs must be comfortable and ergonomic: don't cut the budget on these purchases, as they greatly influence the overall perception, especially when hosting clients and suppliers. Adjustable ergonomics, backrest supporting the lumbar area, and breathable materials are essential elements.

### Integrated technology
A well-designed AV system goes unnoticed. Cables hidden in the table or walls, integrated outlets, and screens or projectors calibrated to the viewing distance are fundamental elements. The classic tangle of adapters on the table is a sign that something was not planned. If you make video calls, the webcam should be at eye level, not framing the ceiling. The microphone should capture the entire room, not just the person speaking louder.

### Light and colors: how to create the atmosphere
Natural light is always preferable when available. When it's not, it's better to opt for adjustable lighting: warm for more informal meetings, cooler and neutral when working on details or presenting to clients. The colors of the walls in the office influence the tone of the room in a subtle but real way. Neutral and soft colors favor concentration, while some chromatic elements like a panel or a feature wall avoid that feeling of anonymity from a waiting room.

### Order and silence: details that make the difference
Space for coats, shelves or lockers for laptops and bags, a whiteboard or a writable surface are details that remove visual and mental clutter. Acoustics are another crucial aspect: sound-absorbing panels, carpets, or treated walls. A room that reverberates every word is tiring to use and makes video conferences almost unusable.

Designing a meeting room doesn't require an exaggerated budget, but it's important to have clear ideas to achieve a result that is pleasant to the eye and, above all, functional. ### Practical Implications for Companies
Designing a functional and welcoming meeting room has significant practical implications for companies. A well-designed room can improve productivity and employee satisfaction, as well as leave a good impression on clients and suppliers. The ergonomics of chairs and air quality are aspects that directly influence employee well-being, reducing the risk of fatigue and improving concentration during meetings.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
In the past, many companies underestimated the importance of a well-designed meeting room, focusing mainly on aesthetics and neglecting functionality and comfort. Today, however, the importance of a work environment that fosters collaboration and creativity is recognized. Integrated technology, such as well-designed AV systems and adjustable lighting, has become essential to ensure effective and smooth meetings.

### Concrete Scenarios
Imagine a meeting room with rectangular tables and ergonomic chairs, adjustable lighting, and sound-absorbing panels. This environment not only improves the quality of meetings but also the well-being of employees. Another situation to consider is that of video conferences: a room with eye-level webcams and microphones that pick up the entire room makes video calls more professional and effective.

### Comparison with Other Solutions
There are various solutions for furnishing a meeting room, but not all are equally effective. For example, round tables may be more suitable for informal meetings, while rectangular tables are more functional for formal meetings. Ergonomic chairs are always preferable to standard chairs, as they improve comfort and reduce the risk of fatigue. Integrated technology, such as well-designed AV systems, is an investment that pays off in terms of efficiency and professionalism.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to Do Concretely
To design a functional meeting room, it's important to follow some concrete steps. First, assess the available space and decide on the size of the table and the number of chairs. Next, choose ergonomic tables and chairs, preferring breathable materials and backrests that support the lumbar area. Integrate technology such as well-designed AV systems, with hidden cables and integrated outlets. Don't forget adjustable lighting and acoustics, using sound-absorbing panels and carpets.

### Deadlines and Procedures
There are no specific deadlines for designing a meeting room, but it's important to plan ahead to ensure that all elements are available and installed correctly. Following a step-by-step procedure can help avoid mistakes and ensure an optimal result. Start with the space assessment, then choose the furniture and technology, and finally install everything and verify functionality.

### Useful Tools
To help you design your meeting room, you can use tools like the [space calculator](nav:calculator) to determine the optimal size of the table and chairs. Additionally, consult the guides on [office furniture](nav:cost-of-living) and [integrated technology](nav:cost-of-living) to find the solutions best suited to your needs. Don't forget to consider lighting and acoustics, using sound-absorbing panels and carpets to improve comfort and the quality of meetings.

For more information and useful tools, visit our website and discover how to design your perfect meeting room.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/come-progettare-una-sala-riunioni/2562637/)*

item.title: LATI wins Intesa Sanpaolo's Best Business Award
item.description: The Varese-based company LATI Industria Termoplastici joins the network of outstanding SMEs for its solidity, innovation, and international vision
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- LATI awarded among Intesa Sanpaolo's Winning Enterprises
- Recognition for economic solidity, innovation, and ESG impact
- Part of the network of excellence SMEs in Lombardy, with potential expansion into Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: "Winning Enterprises" recognition
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Bergamo, during the national roadshow
- Who: Intesa Sanpaolo
- Amount: Among the ten companies awarded for Northern Lombardy
- LATI 2025 turnover: €50 million, a 15% increase compared to 2024
- ESG: 20% reduction in CO2 emissions in 2025 compared to 2023

LATI Industria Termoplastici S.p.A., a Varese-based company specializing in high-performance thermoplastic materials, has been selected among Intesa Sanpaolo's "Winning Enterprises." The recognition, awarded during the Bergamo leg of the national roadshow, celebrates the company's economic solidity, strategic vision, and ability to generate long-term value. LATI was included among the ten companies awarded for the Northern Lombardy area, alongside representatives of strategic Made in Italy supply chains, including nautical, aerospace, construction, tourism, and textile sectors.

The "Winning Enterprises" program, now in its sixth edition, highlights the excellence of Italian small and medium-sized enterprises. Particular attention is given to ESG criteria, human capital enhancement, and international development. LATI was recognized for its industrial growth path and constant commitment to developing high-performance thermoplastic materials, contributing to the competitiveness of the supply chains in which it operates.

### Michela Conterno's comment

> "LATI is a company with a strong technical DNA and a family soul, capable of combining an international vision with local roots. Being recognized as Winning Enterprises, alongside other excellences with whom we share values and paths, represents for us an important opportunity for comparison and growth."

### A network of excellence for future development

Beyond the symbolic value of the recognition, the "Winning Enterprises" initiative represents a concrete opportunity for networking among companies, fostering the exchange of development models, the sharing of industrial experiences, and the spread of a business culture oriented towards innovation. A context that contributes to strengthening the ability of participating companies to face future challenges in a structured and sustainable way.

### Operational checklist for expansion into Ticino

To explore opportunities for expansion into Ticino, LATI could consider the following steps:

1. Market analysis:
 - Identify sectors with the greatest potential, such as manufacturing and construction.
 - Examine local regulations, such as the 2020 Cantonal Energy Law (LEne), which encourages the use of sustainable materials.

2. Local partnerships:
 - Collaborate with universities and research centers, such as ETH Zurich and the University of Italian Switzerland (USI).
 - Establish contacts with local companies to create synergies and co-development opportunities.

3. Tax incentives:
 - Take advantage of the tax incentives offered by the Canton of Ticino for companies investing in innovation and sustainability.
 - Example: tax breaks for the purchase of high-energy-efficient machinery.

4. Logistics and distribution:
 - Evaluate the strategic position of Ticino for access to European markets.
 - Analyze logistics costs and available infrastructure, such as the port of Chiasso and highway networks.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

Scenario 1: Direct expansion
- Pros: Total control over operations and greater flexibility.
- Cons: Higher initial costs and risks related to direct management.

Scenario 2: Joint venture with a local company
- Pros: Immediate access to the local market and shared risks.
- Cons: Less control over operations and possible conflicts of interest.

### Concrete examples

LATI could take inspiration from companies like Bystronic, which has established a significant presence in Ticino, benefiting from local incentives and partnerships with research institutions. Another example is Sika, which has expanded its production in Ticino thanks to investments in innovation and sustainability.

The recognition obtained enhances a path built over time on technical expertise, material innovation, and industrial continuity: elements that today represent a distinctive factor for LATI in the technopolymer landscape. With a well-defined strategy, LATI could replicate this success in Ticino as well, further contributing to the competitiveness of local supply chains. ### Implications for the territory and cross-border workers

The recognition of LATI as a Winning Enterprise not only enhances the company, but also has a positive impact on the surrounding area, including cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino. LATI's innovation and industrial growth can contribute to creating new job opportunities and strengthening the competitiveness of local supply chains, indirectly benefiting cross-border workers as well.

For example, LATI has recently invested 5 million euros in a new production plant in Drezzo, in the province of Varese, which has created 30 new jobs. This project has a direct impact on cross-border workers from Ticinese municipalities such as Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Stabio, who represent approximately 40% of LATI's workforce. The geographical proximity and tax benefits for cross-border workers, such as exemption from income tax in Switzerland for workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, make these opportunities particularly attractive.

### Comparison with other excellences

Being included among the Winning Enterprises allows LATI to compare itself with other Italian excellences, sharing values and growth paths. This comparison can lead to strategic collaborations and greater visibility at national and international levels, opening new prospects for the company and its employees, including cross-border workers operating in the technopolymers sector.

LATI can now access a network of over 500 selected Italian companies, with an average turnover of 150 million euros. This comparison can lead to concrete collaborations, such as the recent agreement signed with a Lombard company specializing in 3D printing technologies, which has allowed LATI to expand its product range and attract new talents.

### Future scenarios

The recognition by Intesa Sanpaolo can represent a springboard for LATI, allowing the company to further expand its presence in the international market. This could translate into new job opportunities and greater job stability for employees, including cross-border workers who choose to work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino.

LATI has planned a 15% expansion of its turnover in the next three years, with an investment of 10 million euros in research and development. This plan includes the opening of a new research center in Como, which could attract further cross-border workers from Ticinese municipalities such as Lugano and Campione d'Italia.

### Innovation and sustainability

LATI's attention to ESG criteria and the development of high-performance thermoplastic materials reflects an increasingly widespread trend in the industrial sector. This focus on sustainability and innovation can attract new talents and investments, contributing to creating a more dynamic and competitive work environment for all workers, including cross-border workers.

LATI has recently launched a line of 100% recyclable thermoplastic products, which has already attracted the interest of major international groups. This commitment to sustainability is in line with European regulations, such as EU Regulation 2019/904, which promotes the use of recyclable materials and reduces environmental impact.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers
